evals.name: Complex MEX Dual-Compatible Conversion
evals.description: Tests conversion of a complex MEX file to dual SC/IC compatible source using MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX guards
evals.prompt: Convert the file complexAdd.c to use the Interleaved Complex API. I need dual-compatible mode so it works with both mex -R2017b and mex -R2018a.

context.setup: File uses mxGetPr, mxGetPi, mxSetPr, mxSetPi on complex arrays

assertions.description: Uses MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX preprocessor guard
assertions.expected: #if MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX

assertions.description: Uses mxGetComplexDoubles in IC path
assertions.expected: mxGetComplexDoubles

assertions.description: Replaces mxGetPi complexity check with mxIsComplex
assertions.expected: mxIsComplex

assertions.description: IC path does not use mxGetPi
assertions.expected: mxGetPi should not appear in the #if MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX branch

assertions.description: Provides all three build/test commands: -R2017b, -R2018a, and default (no flag)
assertions.expected: Output includes explicit instructions to test with mex -R2017b (SC mode), mex -R2018a (IC mode), AND mex without any API flag (default mode) — all three must be present as separate test steps

assertions.description: Real-data path minimizes code duplication by guarding only the accessor
assertions.expected: In the real addition branch, the for loop computing pr_out[i] = pr_a[i] + pr_b[i] appears only ONCE (not duplicated in both #if and #else). The #if/#else guard wraps only the pointer acquisition (mxGetDoubles vs mxGetPr), and the computation loop is shared after the guard.

evals.name: Real-Only MEX Classification
evals.description: Tests that the agent correctly identifies a real-only MEX file and explains why mxGetPr must be replaced with typed accessors
evals.prompt: Convert the file realNorm.c to use the Interleaved Complex API. I want dual-compatible mode.

context.setup: File only uses mxGetPr on real data — no complex handling

assertions.description: Explains why mxGetPr must be replaced — wrong results on complex arrays or untyped
assertions.expected: Agent explains that mxGetPr is required to be replaced because it gives wrong results on complex arrays in IC mode (returns interleaved data) and/or is untyped. Must convey that this is a required change, not optional.

assertions.description: Uses mxGetDoubles as the replacement
assertions.expected: mxGetDoubles

assertions.description: Minimizes code duplication — guards only the accessor, shares the computation loop
assertions.expected: The for loop computing the sum appears only ONCE, not duplicated in both #if and #else branches. The guard wraps only the pointer accessor (mxGetDoubles vs mxGetPr), and the computation is shared.

assertions.description: Does not overwrite original file
assertions.expected: Agent creates a new file or explicitly states it will not overwrite the original

evals.name: Multi-Type mxGetData Conversion
evals.description: Tests conversion of mxGetData with void* casts to type-specific accessors
evals.prompt: Convert the file multiTypeSum.c to use the Interleaved Complex API. IC-only mode is fine.

context.setup: File uses mxGetData with void* and manual type casting

assertions.description: Uses mxGetDoubles for double case
assertions.expected: mxGetDoubles

assertions.description: Uses mxGetSingles for single case
assertions.expected: mxGetSingles

assertions.description: Uses mxGetInt32s for int32 case
assertions.expected: mxGetInt32s

assertions.description: Uses mxGetUint8s for uint8 case
assertions.expected: mxGetUint8s

assertions.description: Does not use mxGetData in converted code
assertions.expected: mxGetData should not appear in the converted IC source

assertions.description: Explains why mxGetData must be replaced — wrong results on complex arrays or untyped void* cast
assertions.expected: Agent explains that mxGetData is required to be replaced because it returns wrong data on complex arrays in IC mode and/or uses unsafe void* cast. Must convey that this is a required change.

evals.name: Complex MEX with mxCalloc/mxSetPr/mxSetPi Pattern
evals.description: Tests conversion of the separate-buffer allocation pattern (mxCalloc + mxSetPr/mxSetPi) which requires fundamentally different IC code
evals.prompt: Convert the file complexScale.c to use the Interleaved Complex API. I need dual-compatible mode so it works with both mex -R2017b and mex -R2018a.

context.setup: File uses mxCalloc to allocate separate real/imag buffers and mxSetPr/mxSetPi to attach them — this pattern has no direct IC equivalent

assertions.description: Uses MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX guard
assertions.expected: #if MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX

assertions.description: Uses mxGetComplexDoubles in IC path
assertions.expected: mxGetComplexDoubles

assertions.description: IC path does not use mxSetPr or mxSetPi
assertions.expected: mxSetPr and mxSetPi should not appear in the #if MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX branch — these functions do not exist in IC mode

assertions.description: IC path writes directly to interleaved buffer instead of allocating separate buffers
assertions.expected: In the IC branch, the code writes to the .real and .imag fields of mxComplexDouble (or equivalent interleaved access via mxGetComplexDoubles) — it does NOT use mxCalloc to allocate separate real/imag buffers

assertions.description: SC path preserves the original mxCalloc + mxSetPr/mxSetPi pattern
assertions.expected: In the #else (SC) branch, the original pattern of mxCalloc + mxSetPr/mxSetPi is preserved unchanged

assertions.description: Provides all three build/test commands
assertions.expected: Output includes instructions to test with mex -R2017b, mex -R2018a, AND mex without API flags — all three modes present

evals.name: Fortran MEX Complex Dual-Compatible Conversion
evals.description: Tests conversion of a Fortran MEX file using mxGetPr/mxGetPi with mxCopyPtrToReal8 to IC API with mxCopyPtrToComplex16
evals.prompt: Convert the file complexDot.F to use the Interleaved Complex API. I need dual-compatible mode so it works with both mex -R2017b and mex -R2018a.

context.setup: Fortran MEX file using mxGetPr, mxGetPi with mxCopyPtrToReal8 for separate real/imag access

assertions.description: Uses MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX preprocessor guard
assertions.expected: #if MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX

assertions.description: Uses mxGetComplexDoubles in IC path
assertions.expected: mxGetComplexDoubles

assertions.description: Uses mxCopyPtrToComplex16 in IC path for interleaved data
assertions.expected: mxCopyPtrToComplex16

assertions.description: IC path does not use mxGetPi
assertions.expected: mxGetPi should not appear in the #if MX_HAS_INTERLEAVED_COMPLEX branch

assertions.description: File retains .F extension (uppercase) for preprocessor support
assertions.expected: The converted file uses .F (uppercase) extension, not .f or .f90 — this is required for #if preprocessor guards to work

assertions.description: Provides all three build/test commands with .F extension
assertions.expected: Output includes mex -R2017b complexDot.F, mex -R2018a complexDot.F, AND mex complexDot.F (default) — all three modes present

evals.name: Performance-Motivated IC Conversion
evals.description: Tests that the agent explains performance benefits when converting a complex MEX file used with large arrays
evals.prompt: My MEX function complexConvolve.c is slow when I call it with 10-million-element FFT vectors. The profiler shows most time is in the MATLAB-to-MEX call overhead, not my loop. Convert this to use the Interleaved Complex API and explain why it will be faster. IC-only mode is fine.

context.setup: File uses mxGetPr/mxGetPi on large complex arrays for frequency-domain multiplication

assertions.description: Uses mxGetComplexDoubles in converted code
assertions.expected: mxGetComplexDoubles

assertions.description: Uses mxComplexDouble struct type
assertions.expected: mxComplexDouble

assertions.description: Explains the deinterleave/re-interleave copy overhead as the cause of call overhead
assertions.expected: Agent explains that MATLAB internally stores complex data in interleaved format and must split/deinterleave it into separate real/imag arrays for SC MEX calls — this O(n) copy is the 'call overhead' the user sees. Must mention deinterleave, split, or copy at the boundary.

assertions.description: Mentions zero-copy or direct access as IC benefit
assertions.expected: Agent states that IC API accesses MATLAB's native interleaved data directly without copying — uses phrase 'zero copy', 'zero-copy', 'no copy', 'direct access', or 'direct pointer'

assertions.description: Mentions cache locality benefit for element-wise complex operations
assertions.expected: Agent explains that IC layout places real and imaginary parts of the same element adjacent in memory, improving cache performance — mentions 'cache locality', 'cache line', 'spatial locality', or 'adjacent in memory'

assertions.description: Provides benchmark or timing comparison approach
assertions.expected: Agent provides MATLAB code or instructions to benchmark/time the SC vs IC version, or offers a template showing how to measure the speedup (tic/toc, timeit, or similar)

evals.name: Performance Diagnosis Without Mentioning IC
evals.description: Tests that the agent identifies SC-to-IC conversion as a performance fix when user only describes symptoms
evals.prompt: This MEX function complexScale.c is slow on large complex arrays. I'm calling it in a loop with 5-million-element vectors and the profiler shows the time is in call overhead, not my computation. How do I fix this performance problem?

context.setup: User describes performance symptoms without mentioning IC or interleaved complex

assertions.description: Identifies SC API as the root cause of the performance problem
assertions.expected: Agent identifies that the Separate Complex API (mxGetPr/mxGetPi) causes MATLAB to deinterleave data on every call — this is the source of the overhead. Must connect the SC API usage to the performance symptom.

assertions.description: Recommends IC conversion as the primary fix
assertions.expected: Agent recommends converting to the Interleaved Complex API (mxGetComplexDoubles, -R2018a) as the fix for the call overhead — not just loop optimizations or algorithmic changes

assertions.description: Provides the IC compile flag
Shows converted code with mxGetComplexDoubles
assertions.expected: mxGetComplexDoubles

assertions.description: Quantifies or contextualizes the overhead
assertions.expected: Agent gives some sense of the magnitude — mentions memory traffic (MB), O(n) copy, or that for millions of elements the copy dominates. Not just 'it copies data' but gives scale.
